content: I've been thinking about getting more serious about fitness, especially with my wedding coming up next year. Working at SpaceX is great but the hours are insane.
Congratulations on your upcoming wedding! What kind of fitness activities have you enjoyed in the past?
I used to do a lot of yoga and hiking. My fiancé and I actually met while hiking in the San Gabriel Mountains. But lately I've been so busy with this rocket project that I barely move during the day.
That's a beautiful story! How about we find some ways to incorporate movement into your busy schedule? Even short breaks can help.
I'd love that. I've been trying to learn Rust programming in my spare time too, which means even more sitting. My posture is terrible these days.
Ah, the programmer's posture! Have you considered a standing desk or taking short walking breaks every hour?
I actually just got a standing desk last month, but I'm not used to it yet. I find myself wanting to sit after about 30 minutes. Maybe I need to build up gradually.
That's exactly right - your body needs time to adjust. What about using part of your lunch break for a quick walk? The SpaceX campus has some great walking paths.
It really does. Sometimes I bring my lunch outside and just sit and watch the rockets being tested. It's oddly calming.
That sounds amazing! Combining mindfulness with a bit of movement could be perfect. Have you tried any of the SpaceX wellness programs?
I've looked into them but haven't committed yet. Between calligraphy classes, wedding planning, and trying to perfect my pad thai recipe, I'm not sure where fitness fits.
You have such diverse interests! What if we found ways to combine them? Like listening to calligraphy podcasts while on the treadmill?
That's actually not a bad idea. I could also practice my Japanese origami folds while doing some light stretching. My grandmother taught me when I was little.
That's a lovely connection to your heritage! How about we start with just 10 minutes of movement each day, paired with one of your hobbies?
I think I can manage that. Maybe I'll start tomorrow morning - I have a vintage car meetup on Saturday that I've been looking forward to, so I want to feel good for that.
Perfect timing! Starting fresh on a weekday and having that weekend activity as motivation is a great plan. Let's check in on Friday to see how your first few days went.
